title: Site Init
content:
This has been an idea of mine for quite some time. I have a deep love of 90's and early 2000 media. This includes cyberpunk, anime, music, and general technology of the time.
Ideally this wouldn't just be a site where I am gushing over Lain and the Matrix movies over and over again, but we shall see. The idea is that I'll write about things that I really enjoy, and maybe thoughts on how they have evolved in cultural context over time.

title: Babylon 5
content: I have been watching Babylon 5 recently, mostly because I haven't seen it since the 90's, and I wanted to see if it has aged well. I feel like it had a bad reputation at the time, especially since on the surface it seemed to have been a Star Trek DS9 rip off. The first season was pretty rough, and the acting is not particularly great. However, I feel that starting on season 2 it really picked up as something worth watching. It dealt with themes and concepts that was out of scope of the type of show Star Trek wanted to be. I think the costumes still stand up really well, and some really cool looking alien designs especially for a TV show. I always liked the look of the Starfury ships, and they still look cool today, although they didn't play as large of a role as I remembered. I am surprised how much i love Londo Mollari's character, despite looking absolutely silly, he has moments which are captivating. He has a character arch which is painful to watch, but absolutely satisfying. Looking at the show now, I am shocked at how topical the themes of fascism still ring today.
